pi_items.source: Verified in task.json description (line 5), setup_task.sh (line 24), and export_result.sh (line 19). All scripts search for and create records matching '%Cyberbullying%'.

Table name confirmed in setup_task.sh (lines 23-24) and export_result.sh (line 18-19). However, this table is NOT in the official OpenSIS v9.2 schema (verified by downloading and inspecting OpensisSchemaMysqlInc.sql from OS4ED/openSIS-Classic GitHub). The table is custom-created for this benchmark task.

No evidence found. Searched all task files, environment scripts, OpenSIS official schema files, and GitHub repositories. The field 'usage_key' is not mentioned anywhere in setup_task.sh, export_result.sh, verifier.py, or in the official OpenSIS schema (OpensisSchemaMysqlInc.sql, OpensisSchemaMysqlIncUpdate.sql, OpensisUpdateSchemaMysql.sql from https://github.com/OS4ED/openSIS-Classic). Also checked trash_tasks/opensis_env/add_discipline_referral/setup_task.sh which creates discipline_field_usage with only 'title' and 'sort_order' fields visible. No 'OFFENSE' value is referenced anywhere.

privileged_info_summary: The task requires adding a record with title 'Cyberbullying' to a custom table called 'discipline_field_usage' in a local OpenSIS installation. The table is not part of the standard OpenSIS v9.2 schema but is created for this benchmark task. The metadata claim about 'usage_key' field containing 'OFFENSE' could not be verified and has no supporting evidence in any scripts, code, or documentation.
